description: Perform a multidimensional scaling analysis of roll call votes from the 117th U.S. Senate to quantify political polarization. Using member data (https://voteview.com/static/data/out/members/S117_members.csv) and voting data (https://voteview.com/static/data/out/votes/S117_votes.csv), estimate legislator ideal points in two dimensions where Dimension 1 captures the primary Liberal-Conservative axis. Process the voting data into a roll call matrix using a simplified binary format (1, 2, 3 = Yea; 4, 5, 6 = Nay; 7, 8, 9 = NA). Save the estimated ideal points to /home/ga/RProjects/output/senate_ideal_points.csv with columns for 'icpsr', 'name', 'party_code', 'dim1', and 'dim2'. Create a scatter plot visualization of Dim1 vs Dim2 colored by party, including labels for Senators Manchin, Sanders, and McConnell, saved to /home/ga/RProjects/output/polarization_map.png. Write the R code in /home/ga/RProjects/senate_analysis.R.
